patient_information.insurance: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Oklahoma - Medicare Advantage PPO

diagnosis_and_clinical_information.allergies: Allergic to penicillin (developed generalized pruritic rash and hives requiring urgent care visit in her 20s; avoids all penicillin-class antibiotics). Reports nausea and vomiting with codeine-containing pain medications. Seasonal allergic rhinitis triggered by ragweed and grass pollens, managed with over-the-counter cetirizine as needed. No known allergies to contrast dye, latex, or biologic medications.
diagnosis_and_clinical_information.therapies_tried_and_failed:
1) Alendronate 70 mg PO once weekly (Fosamax) from 2017-2019: discontinued due to significant gastrointestinal side effects including epigastric pain and worsening reflux symptoms despite taking with full glass of water and remaining upright for 30+ minutes.
2) Risedronate 35 mg PO once weekly (Actonel) from 2019-2021: tolerated somewhat better than alendronate but still experienced intermittent upper GI discomfort and poor adherence; follow-up DEXA in 2021 showed continued decline in bone mineral density at the lumbar spine.
3) Denosumab 60 mg SQ every 6 months (Prolia) from 2021-2023: received a total of four injections. Initial response with slight improvement in BMD, but therapy discontinued after she developed persistent musculoskeletal pain in hips and thighs that she strongly associated with the injections, along with insurance coverage issues leading to delays between doses. Most recent DEXA in 2023 again showed T-score in the osteoporosis range at the lumbar spine and femoral neck despite prior therapies.
diagnosis_and_clinical_information.tb_test_results: Negative interferon-gamma release assay (Quantiferon Gold) with no evidence of latent or active tuberculosis infection.
diagnosis_and_clinical_information.hep_b_test_results: Hepatitis B surface antigen (HBsAg) negative, Hepatitis B surface antibody (HBsAb) positive at protective titer consistent with prior immunization, Hepatitis B core antibody (HBcAb) negative.

lab_orders.lab_orders_other: Serum 25-hydroxy Vitamin D level prior to initiation and periodically during therapy to ensure adequacy of supplementation.

pre_medication_orders.pre_med_other: Encourage patient to eat a light meal 1–2 hours prior to injection visit to reduce risk of lightheadedness. Ensure patient has taken her usual morning medications, including antihypertensives, unless otherwise instructed by her primary care provider.

standing_orders_for_adverse_reactions.standing_other: Monitor vital signs (blood pressure, heart rate, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation) at baseline, 15 minutes after administration, and prior to discharge, or more frequently if clinically indicated. Observe patient on-site for at least 30 minutes after Evenity injection for any signs of hypersensitivity or other acute reaction.
